title: Why some cafés become writing offices
summary: Explore why certain cafés attract people who come to work, think, or write, and what qualities make a space feel productive.
topic: Why some cafés become writing offices
prompt: Write 3 paragraphs (350–500 words) exploring why certain cafés attract writers, students, and remote workers. Describe a specific real or imagined café and what makes it feel like a working space. Use present perfect for ongoing observations, at least one sentence with 'although' or 'however', a second-conditional sentence, and modal verbs to discuss possibility.
hintStarters: There is a café in my city that is always full of people staring at laptops, and I have spent hours there trying to understand what makes it…
I'd argue that the best working café is not the one with the fastest Wi-Fi but the one that…
If you designed a café purely for productivity, you would probably remove everything that actually makes people stay…

hintVocab.meaningVi: bầu không khí làm việc hiệu quả
an vị, cảm thấy thoải mái ở chỗ mới
ngăn những thứ gây xao nhãng
tiếng ồn trắng (âm thanh đều đều)
ở lại lâu hơn mức chào đón

sampleText:
There is a café near the main square in Brno, in the Czech Republic, that has been drawing writers and students for decades. The tables are mismatched, the chairs are wooden and slightly uncomfortable, and the music — when there is any — is quiet enough to ignore. On any afternoon, you can find at least a dozen people working there. I have spent many hours at a corner table with a notebook, and I have never once felt the urge to leave early. What the café has managed to create, without appearing to try, is an atmosphere where thinking feels possible.

Although most people assume they need silence to concentrate, research and common sense both suggest otherwise. A low, steady layer of ambient noise — the click of cups, the hum of conversation, the occasional door — seems to help the brain stay engaged without pulling its focus in any single direction. The café also offers something the home office usually cannot: a sense of anonymity. You are surrounded by strangers who are equally absorbed in their own work, which creates a kind of collective permission to be occupied. Nobody is waiting for you to make conversation. If you designed a working space purely on the basis of productivity research, you would probably arrive at something that looks very much like a good café: moderate noise, natural light, a reason to stay seated, and a mild social presence that keeps isolation from creeping in.

However, not every café earns this function. The ones that become informal offices tend to share a few quiet characteristics. They do not hurry you. The staff may refill your water without being asked, but they do not check in every ten minutes. The seating is spread out enough that you can have a table to yourself. The Wi-Fi is reliable but not advertised, because the kind of person who needs to be told there is Wi-Fi is probably not the kind of person who will settle in for three hours. These details may seem minor, but together they send a clear signal: you are welcome to stay, and your presence here has a purpose. That signal is what turns a café into a working space, and once a place earns that reputation, it tends to keep it.

đủ nhẹ để không chú ý đến
sự thôi thúc rời đi sớm
mà không có vẻ cố gắng
việc suy nghĩ cảm thấy khả thi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a low, steady layer of ambient noise
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một lớp tiếng ồn nền thấp và đều đặn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: pulling its focus in any single direction
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: kéo sự tập trung theo một hướng cụ thể

sampleAnnotations.phrase: equally absorbed in their own work
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đều đang chìm đắm vào công việc của họ
sự cho phép tập thể để bận rộn
ngăn sự cô đơn len lỏi vào
có xu hướng duy trì nó
sự hiện diện xã hội nhẹ nhàng

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present perfect continuous for long-term patterns
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'There is a café near the main square in Brno that has been drawing writers and students for decades' dùng hiện tại hoàn thành tiếp diễn để mô tả một hoạt động đang diễn ra liên tục từ quá khứ đến hiện tại. Nó nhấn mạnh tính liên tục hơn là kết quả.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'There is a café near the main square in Brno that has been drawing writers and students for decades' uses the present perfect continuous to describe an ongoing pattern from the past to now, emphasising continuity rather than completion.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Second conditional to reason from evidence
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'If you designed a working space purely on the basis of productivity research, you would probably arrive at something that looks very much like a good café' dùng quá khứ đơn + would để trình bày một kết luận lý thuyết — điều này ngụ ý rằng những quán cà phê tốt đã tự nhiên đạt được điều mà khoa học gợi ý.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'If you designed a working space purely on the basis of productivity research, you would probably arrive at something that looks very much like a good café' uses past simple + would to present a theoretical conclusion — implying that good cafés have naturally arrived at what science suggests.

sampleTranslationVi:
Có một quán cà phê gần quảng trường chính ở Brno, Cộng hòa Séc, đã thu hút các nhà văn và sinh viên trong nhiều thập kỷ. Những chiếc bàn không đồng bộ, ghế bằng gỗ và hơi không thoải mái, và âm nhạc — khi có — đủ nhẹ để không chú ý đến. Vào bất kỳ buổi chiều nào, bạn có thể tìm thấy ít nhất một chục người đang làm việc ở đó. Tôi đã trải qua nhiều giờ tại một chiếc bàn góc với quyển sổ tay, và tôi chưa bao giờ cảm thấy sự thôi thúc rời đi sớm. Điều mà quán cà phê đã tạo ra, mà không có vẻ cố gắng, là một bầu không khí nơi việc suy nghĩ cảm thấy khả thi.

Mặc dù hầu hết mọi người cho rằng họ cần yên tĩnh để tập trung, nghiên cứu và lẽ thường đều gợi ý điều khác. Một lớp tiếng ồn nền thấp và đều đặn — tiếng leng keng của cốc, tiếng ồn của cuộc trò chuyện, đôi khi tiếng cửa — dường như giúp não bộ duy trì sự tham gia mà không kéo sự tập trung theo một hướng cụ thể. Quán cà phê cũng cung cấp điều mà văn phòng tại nhà thường không thể: cảm giác ẩn danh. Bạn bị bao quanh bởi những người lạ cũng đang chìm đắm vào công việc của họ, điều này tạo ra một loại sự cho phép tập thể để bận rộn. Không ai đang chờ đợi bạn bắt đầu trò chuyện. Nếu bạn thiết kế một không gian làm việc thuần túy dựa trên nghiên cứu năng suất, bạn có lẽ sẽ đến được thứ gì đó trông rất giống một quán cà phê tốt: tiếng ồn vừa phải, ánh sáng tự nhiên, lý do để ngồi yên, và một sự hiện diện xã hội nhẹ nhàng ngăn sự cô đơn len lỏi vào.

Tuy nhiên, không phải mọi quán cà phê đều kiếm được chức năng này. Những quán trở thành văn phòng không chính thức có xu hướng chia sẻ một vài đặc điểm yên lặng. Họ không thúc giục bạn. Nhân viên có thể rót thêm nước cho bạn mà không cần hỏi, nhưng họ không kiểm tra mỗi mười phút. Chỗ ngồi được sắp xếp đủ rộng rãi để bạn có thể có một chiếc bàn cho riêng mình. Wi-Fi đáng tin cậy nhưng không được quảng bá, bởi vì loại người cần được thông báo rằng có Wi-Fi có lẽ không phải là loại người sẽ an vị trong ba tiếng. Những chi tiết này có vẻ nhỏ nhặt, nhưng cùng nhau chúng gửi đi một tín hiệu rõ ràng: bạn được chào đón ở lại, và sự hiện diện của bạn ở đây có mục đích. Tín hiệu đó là thứ biến một quán cà phê thành không gian làm việc, và một khi một nơi kiếm được danh tiếng đó, nó có xu hướng duy trì nó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the present perfect continuous?
options: The café is drawing writers and students for decades.
The café has drawn writers and students since decades.
The café was drawing writers and students for decades.
The café has been drawing writers and students for decades.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect continuous 'has been + -ing' + 'for' describes a continuing action from the past to now.
mcQuestions.hint: Use 'has been + -ing + for' when the action is still happening now.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the second conditional correctly?
options: If you designed a working space on the basis of productivity research, you would arrive at something like a good café.
If you will design a working space on the basis of productivity research, you would arrive at something like a good café.
If you designed a working space on the basis of productivity research, you will arrive at something like a good café.
If you would design a working space on the basis of productivity research, you would arrive at something like a good café.
mcQuestions.explanation: Second conditional: past simple in the if-clause + 'would + bare infinitive' in the main clause. Never 'would' in the if-clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Use past simple after 'if' and 'would + base verb' in the main clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'although' to introduce a concession correctly?
options: Although most people assume they need silence to concentrate, but research suggests otherwise.
Although most people assume they need silence to concentrate, research suggests otherwise.
Although, most people assume they need silence to concentrate, research suggests otherwise.
Most people assume they need silence, although research suggests otherwise however.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Although + concession clause, main clause' — no 'but' and no comma after 'although'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Although' introduces the subordinate clause; the main clause follows directly.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which modal best expresses possibility based on observation?
options: A low layer of ambient noise must help the brain stay engaged.
A low layer of ambient noise will help the brain stay engaged.
A low layer of ambient noise seems to help the brain stay engaged.
A low layer of ambient noise should help the brain stay engaged.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Seems to' expresses a careful observation or conclusion based on evidence, without claiming certainty.
mcQuestions.hint: Use 'seems to' when drawing a tentative conclusion from what you observe.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'however' correctly as a discourse marker?
options: However not every café earns this function.
Not every café however, earns this function.
Not every café earns, however this function.
However, not every café earns this function.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'However' at the start of a sentence must be followed by a comma.
mcQuestions.hint: Sentence-initial 'however' always takes a comma after it.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase correctly describes background sound in a working space?
options: ambient noise
background sound
surrounding noise
environmental sounds
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Ambient noise' is the standard collocation for background sound in an environment.
mcQuestions.hint: This is a common collocation — only one adjective typically combines with 'noise' in this context.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses a relative clause correctly after 'something'?
options: You would arrive at something which it looks very much like a good café.
You would arrive at something that looks very much like a good café.
You would arrive at something that it looks very much like a good café.
You would arrive at something looks very much like a good café.
mcQuestions.explanation: After 'something that', use a verb directly — do not add a pronoun ('it') to repeat the subject.
mcQuestions.hint: In relative clauses, 'that' replaces the subject — no need to add 'it'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly uses 'enough' with an adjective?
options: The music is enough quiet to ignore.
The music is quietly enough to ignore.
The music is quiet enough to ignore.
The music is enough quietly to ignore.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Adjective + enough + to + infinitive' is the correct structure. 'Enough' comes after the adjective.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Enough' always follows the adjective: 'quiet enough', not 'enough quiet'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses parallel structure correctly in a list?
options: moderate noise, natural light, to stay seated for a reason, and a mild social presence
moderate noise, naturally lit, a reason to stay seated, and mild socially present
moderate noise, light that is natural, a reason to stay seated, and a mild social presence
moderate noise, natural light, a reason to stay seated, and a mild social presence
mcQuestions.explanation: All items should share the same grammatical form. Here, all are noun phrases: 'moderate noise', 'natural light', 'a reason...', 'a mild social presence'.
mcQuestions.hint: Keep the same structure across all items in the list.

options: Once a place earns that reputation, it tends to keep it.
Once a place earns that reputation, it is tending to keep it.
Once a place earns that reputation, it tends keeping it.
Once a place earns that reputation, it tends to keeping it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Tend to + bare infinitive' is the correct structure. Never 'tend to + gerund' or 'tending to'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Tend to' is always followed by the base form of the verb.

options: The café created this atmosphere without to appear to try.
The café created this atmosphere without appearing to try.
The café created this atmosphere without appear to try.
The café created this atmosphere without it appearing trying.
mcQuestions.explanation: Prepositions like 'without' are always followed by a gerund (-ing form), not a bare infinitive or 'to + verb'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'without', use the -ing form of the verb.

criticalThinkingQuestion: If cafés help people think and write more clearly, what does that suggest about the home and the office? Can those spaces be redesigned to have the same effect, or is the café's power tied to the fact that you chose to go there?
